content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The client begins firmly in the fearless/logistical framing ('fear isn't really my deal... it's more like a logistical thing') and a smooth 'fine' surface. By turn 11 and 13, genuine micro-shifts appear in the client's own language: noticing 'I just said fine twice,' the unprompted 'Huh. That's a strange way to put it,' and the description of fine as 'the water I swim in... the absence of a problem.' This is exactly the numbness layer beginning to become felt — the client is, for the first time, paying attention to the texture of 'fine.' However, the conversation ends just as this opens, and the coach's final move (turn 14, 'What would it mean if it were more than fine?') pulls away from the texture toward hypothetical meaning, so we don't see the client stay with or deepen the felt quality. The breakthrough condition was approached and arguably touched briefly ('huh, that IS something') but not entered or stabilized. That's a textbook 2: partial movement, deeper layer brushed but not inhabited.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 9, 11, 13],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Baseline: fearless self-concept, framed as logistics/discipline. Smooth, articulate surface."},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Still on surface; 'we always do,' small smile, no felt contact."},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "First hint of the numbness: 'I just sort of drift... I'm just elsewhere... I don't really notice the transition.' Client describing dissociation without naming it."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Small somatic/affective shift noted by client himself: 'something is slightly quieter,' then pulls back to 'checked the boxes.' Even asks if he's missing what coach is pointing at — a crack in the smooth surface."},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Client notices his own pattern: 'I notice I just said fine twice.' Self-observation of the very word that is the gateway to the numbness layer."},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Closest the client gets to the breakthrough: 'fine is the water I swim in... the absence of a problem... Huh. That's a strange way to put it.' The wry 'huh' marker is present, but only just."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Coach pivots to hypothetical ('what would it mean if it were more than fine') rather than staying with the texture of 'fine' that just opened, so we don't see the client deepen into it."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["the-happiness-filter", "disassociating-in-the-coaching-room"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["meet-resistance-with-okay", "lets-go-there", "fear-in-body-vs-fear-in-head", "how-is-my-anxiety-right-now", "disassociation-as-numbness-layer"],
  "notes": "Real movement is visible in client turns 11 and 13 — the 'huh' and the noticing of 'fine' as a texture — but the conversation cuts off before that recognition is stayed with. Scoring on outcome alone: partial, not full."
}
